causeCollection.description:
A lower-level set of structured errors.

Only oneOf `causeWrapped`, `causeSimple`, `causeCollection`, or `causeIndexedCollection` will ever be set.

causeIndexedCollection.description:
A lower-level set of structured errors.
Each of these errors MUST have index set.

Only oneOf `causeWrapped`, `causeSimple`, `causeCollection`, or `causeIndexedCollection` will ever be set.

causeIsInternal.description: If true, then the cause (`causeWrapped`, `causeSimple`, `causeCollection`, or `causeIndexedCollection`) should be hidden from the user.

causeSimple.description:
Simple string error type.

Only oneOf `causeWrapped`, `causeSimple`, `causeCollection`, or `causeIndexedCollection` will ever be set.

domain.description:
The "domain" for the error.  If empty, it is an EDA
core error.  Alternatively it can be an EDA application
"apiVersion" value (e.g. interfaces.eda.nokia.com/v1alpha1)
indicating that the error is specific to that application.
The domain gives the receiver information that they can use
to help them interpret the "type" field.

index.description:
When processing an array, errors may be generated related to a particular array item.
If set, this index indicates to which array item the error applies.

message.description: The basic text error message for the error response.

ref.description: Reference to the error source. Should typically be the URI of the request.

type.description:
Type defines a unique identifier for the error, within the domain.
This may be used (along with the domain) to find an internationalization translation for the message.

SHOULD be a valid golang identifier, and SHOULD be in UpperCamelCase.

values.description:
Associated data/information.
The error "message" may contain {{name}} escapes that should be substituted with information from this dictionary.

Note that this map MUST NOT contain JSON objects (`{...}`) or arrays (`[...]`), only simple JSON types are permitted.

ErrorResponse.title: Generic error response for REST APIs.

K8SPatchOp.description: The definition for a JSON patch for a resource, as in RFC 6902

from.description:
Only applies to a "move" operation, and is the path to the field/value within
the target JSON object that should be moved to the location specified by the "path"
field.

op.description: The type of operation this patch should perform.

path.description:
The path to the field within the target JSON object
that should be patched according to the "op" field.

value.description:
This field would be set to the value which the user intends to put in the
field referenced by the "path" field.  The type of the value should match
the type of the field as indicated by the schema of the resource being
patched.

x-permissive.description:
For an "add" operation, an already-present field referenced by the "path" will
result in an error unless this field is true.  Similarly for a non-existent
target field of a "remove" operation.

Status.title: Status is a return value for calls that don't return other objects.

com.nokia.eda.aifabrics.v1.Backend.description: Backend is the Schema for the backends API

x-eda-nokia-com.ui-pattern-error: The apiVersion must be set to aifabrics.eda.nokia.com/v1
The kind must be set to Backend

spec.description: BackendSpec defines the desired state of Backend

addressAllocation.description: Address allocation profile for GPU endpoints.

leafIndexPoolScope.description: Leaf Index Pool Allocation scope (used for IP Address allocation). Global scope means that the leaf index will be allocated from a common pool for the entire namespace, Fabric scope means that the leaf index will be allocated from a pool for the entire fabric, Stripe scope means that the leaf index will be allocated from a pool for the stripe.
leafIndexPoolScope.title: Leaf Index Pool Scope

name.description: Reference to an IPv6 allocation pool to use for prefix allocation.
name.title: IPv6 Pool Name

addressAllocation.title: Address Allocation

asnPool.description: Reference to an IndexAllocationPool pool to use for Autonomous System Number allocations.  Used when eBGP is configured as an underlay protocol.
asnPool.title: Autonomous System Pool

gpuIsolationGroups.description: GPU Isolation Groups are used to isolate GPU traffic over the network, GPUs in different GPU isolation groups will not be able to communicate with each other.  If all GPUs across all stripes need to be able to communicate with each other, create a single GPUIsolationGroup selecting all GPU facing interfaces.

ipv6Pool.description: IPv6 Pool reference for allocating IPv6 addresses to GPU facing interfaces in this IsolationGroup. Can be used only when `PerTenantIPv6Pool` address allocation type is selected in the Backend spec.

name.description: Reference to an IPv6 allocation pool to use for prefix allocation.
name.title: IPv6 Pool Name

ipv6Pool.title: IPv6 Pool

gpuIsolationGroups.title: GPU Isolation Groups

ipMTU.description: IP MTU for this fabric. Default is 4136 bytes.
ipMTU.title: IP MTU

rocev2QoS.description: Set of properties to configure the RoCEv2 QoS.

ecnMaxDropProbabilityPercent.description: If the queue depth is between min and max threshold then this the probability with which packets are dropped or marked.
ecnMaxDropProbabilityPercent.title: ECN Max Drop Probability Percent

ecnSlopeMaxThresholdPercent.description: The maximum threshold parameter for a RED-managed queue in percent. When the average queue length exceeds the max value, all packets are dropped (or marked if ECN is enabled). Mutually exclusive with min-threshold and max-threshold.
ecnSlopeMaxThresholdPercent.title: ECN Max Threshold Percent

ecnSlopeMinThresholdPercent.description: The minimum threshold parameter for a RED-managed queue in percent. When the average queue length is less than min, all packets are admitted to the queue. Mutually exclusive with min-threshold and max-threshold.
ecnSlopeMinThresholdPercent.title: ECN Min Threshold Percent

pfcDeadlockDetectionTimerMs.description: Number of milliseconds during which outgoing interface is receiving pfc-pause-frames before triggering recovery-timer.
pfcDeadlockDetectionTimerMs.title: PFC Deadlock Detection Timer

pfcDeadlockRecoveryTimerMs.description: Number of milliseconds during which the pfc-pause-frames will be ignored.
pfcDeadlockRecoveryTimerMs.title: PFC Deadlock Recovery Timer

queueMaximumBurstSizeBytes.description: Maximum amount of shared buffer memory available to the queue in bytes.
queueMaximumBurstSizeBytes.title: Maximum Burst Size

rocev2QoS.title: RoCEv2 QoS

stripeConnector.description: StripeConnector is the spine layer interconnecting multiple stripes.

asnPool.description: Reference to an IndexAllocationPool pool to use for Autonomous System Number allocations.
asnPool.title: Autonomous System Pool

linkSelectors.description: Selects TopoLinks to include in this AI Fabric, the selected TopoLinks will be used to create ISLs between the stripe connector devices and the leaf devices.
linkSelectors.title: Link Selector

name.description: The name of the Stripe Connector.
name.title: Stripe Connector Name

nodeSelectors.description: Node selector to select the nodes to be used for this stripe connector.
nodeSelectors.title: Node Selector

systemPoolIPv4.description: Reference to an IPAllocationPool used to dynamically allocate an IPv4 address to system/lo0 interfaces for the stripe connector devices.  If not specified, the system will use the default IPAllocationPool.
systemPoolIPv4.title: IPv4 Pool - System IP

stripeConnector.title: Stripe Connector

stripes.description: A list of stripes, stripes contain a set of nodes (rails).

asnPool.description: Optional reference to an IndexAllocationPool pool to use for Autonomous System Number allocations.  If left blank, ASN allocation will be done from the ASNAllocationRange.
asnPool.title: Autonomous System Pool

gpuVLAN.description: The VLAN used on interfaces facing the GPU servers.
gpuVLAN.title: GPU VLAN

nodeSelectors.description: Node selector to select the nodes to be used for this stripe.
nodeSelectors.title: Node Selector

systemPoolIPv4.description: Optional reference to an IPAllocationPool used to dynamically allocate an IPv4 address to system/lo0 interfaces. If left blank, system IP allocation will be done from the SystemIPV4Subnet.
systemPoolIPv4.title: IPv4 Pool - System IP

stripes.title: Stripes

systemPoolIPv4.description: Reference to an IPAllocationPool used to dynamically allocate an IPv4 address to system/lo0 interfaces.  If specified under the Leaf/Spine/Superspine/Borderleaf those will take precedence.
systemPoolIPv4.title: IPv4 Pool - System IP

spec.title: Specification

status.description: BackendStatus defines the observed state of Backend

health.description: Indicates the health score of the Fabric.  The health score of the Fabric is determined by the aggregate health score of the resources emitted by the Fabric such as ISL, DefaultRouteReflectors etc.
health.title: Health

healthScoreReason.description: Indicates the reason for the health score.
healthScoreReason.title: Health Score Reason

lastChangeTime.description: The time when the state of the resource last changed.
lastChangeTime.title: Last Change

operationalState.description: Operational state of the Fabric.  The operational state of the fabric is determined by monitoring the operational state of the following resources (if applicable): DefaultRouters, ISLs.
operationalState.title: Operational State

name.description: The name of the Stripe Connector.
name.title: Stripe Connector Name

stripeConnectorNodes.description: List of stripe connector nodes in the Stripe.

operatingSystem.description: Operating system running on the node.
